summary: ISS-1487 (c5 H3, moderate — Huber & Rust 2016 + Giacomin & Levinson 2015 cited as supporting 'sub-1% mean-ratio prediction error / single free parameter' but neither paper reports that) and ISS-1488 (c5 H4, moderate — possible circularity: if that sub-1% is in-sample against the same road-network data, the residual is tautological) are jointly resolved by EXP-240, a single sentence-scope substring replacement on WIN-069.detail_evidence. BEFORE-string is the offending defense-paragraph sentence added by EXP-231 (integrated 2026-04-23T00:21Z); occurrence count verified = 1. AFTER-string: (1) correctly characterizes Giacomin & Levinson 2015 as descriptive circuity statistics (~1.19 mean across 51 US MSAs) and Huber & Rust 2016 as the OSRM Stata-tooling paper — both verified via web search against SAGE, EconPapers, Semantic Scholar, ResearchGate on 2026-04-23; (2) adds live DOI links for both papers; (3) explicitly retracts the 'sub-1%' and 'single free parameter' overreach (phrases appear ONLY inside quoted-retraction clauses in the new text); (4) reframes the surviving comparison on parity grounds — V13's 10.2% is itself an in-sample figure, so the in-sample comparison is apples-to-apples and V13 loses before any out-of-sample test is attempted. Surviving core argument is preserved per c5 curmudgeon's explicit guidance ('V13 10.2% mean error remains the stronger point regardless'). Projected detail_evidence length delta: +1092 chars (current 5716 → new 6808). No TLDR / detail_verdict_text / finding / code_analysis changes (all verified clean by string search — none contain 'sub-1%' or 'single free parameter'). Out of scope: c5 major holes #1 (R Journal venue — already resolved in current data) and #2 (finding factual/geography error — decider to queue separately); c5 minor carryovers #5 (thousands-of-pairs) and #6 (0.4% loose bound). Recommended action: patch (apply EXP-240 sentence-scope replacement; close ISS-1487 + ISS-1488 on integration).
